title: Specialized Information and Referral * Disabilities Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Specialized Information and Referral * Disabilities Issues 27 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Assistive Technology Issues Blindness Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Deafness Disabilities Issues Down Syndrome Mental Health Issues Older Adult/Aging Issues Immigration Issues Abused Lesbians/Gay Individuals Abused Transgender Individuals LGBTQ2+ Issues Transgender Individuals Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Native American Community AIDS/HIV Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ACCESS OUTREACH TO SENIORS AND PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outreach to offer specialized support to vulnerable community members, helping them live independently in safe and healthy environments. Through home visits and referrals, the program assesses living situations, identifies needs, and connects individuals with appropriate resources. Assistance may include information, referrals to community partners or other agencies, and occasional emergency services. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 779-6691 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARC PORTLAND METRO CLUB IMPACT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral assistance, weekly social activities, and educational programming to support quality of life. 6929 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Specialized information and referral: Unrestricted All other services: Age 18 and older who have intellectual or developmental disabilities Hours: Monday 6pm-8pm Email (360) 726-3033 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARC UMATILLA COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides connections to resources such as case management services, assistance with advocacy, disability awareness training, education, recreational programs, and vocational training. 215 W Orchard Avenue Hermiston, OR 978381738 Eligibility: Individuals who have intellectual and developmental disabilities and their families Hours: Monday 9am-noon Email (541) 567-7615 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK BEND OFFICE DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational parenting support, family support group sessions, education and mentoring support, and referral to other resources as needed. This empowers individuals with disabilities, and their families, to take charge of their life's direction. Also assists in determining supports, goals, and community inclusion, resulting in increased independence. 2525 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 7 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK KLAMATH FALLS OFFICE DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational parenting support, family support group sessions, education and mentoring support, and referral to other resources as needed. This empowers individuals with disabilities, and their families, to take charge of their life's direction. Also assists in determining supports, goals, and community inclusion, resulting in increased independence. 2701 Foothills Boulevard Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK WARM SPRINGS OFFICE DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational parenting support, family support group sessions, education and mentoring support, and referral to other resources as needed. This empowers individuals with disabilities, and their families, to take charge of their life's direction. Also assists in determining supports, goals, and community inclusion, resulting in increased independence. 1257 Kot-Num Road Warm Springs, OR 97761 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND 311 CITY AND COUNTY INFORMATION AND REFERRAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Information and referral services to all City of Portland Bureaus, Multnomah County Departments, and other local government services. Also provides information about disability rights and resolves issues about ADA accessibility for the City of Portland. 1120 SW 5th Avenue Suite 114 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone or email: 7 days per week 7am-8pm (closed for federal holidays) Email (503) 823-4000 311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLEARVIEW DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with general advocacy and locating community resources. 1114 SW Frazer Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 276-1130 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DIRECTION SERVICE INFORMATION AND REFERRAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral support for disability-related topics. Maintains a clearinghouse of local resources available to youth and adults with disabilities. Creates a yearly resource guide for families, which includes activities, camps, community supports, educational supports, and employment programs. 1144 Gateway Loop Suite 200 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 686-5060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DISABILITY RIGHTS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy and limited legal services to people with disabilities. DRO's individual advocacy focuses on providing callers with information and materials about their rights to help them advocate for themselves. Occasionally, Disability Rights Oregon will engage in litigation to protect the rights of Oregonians under applicable federal and state laws. DRO does not provide the following services: -Representation in criminal or family law matters -Help finding employment, housing, or financial assistance -Case management -Assistance for short term disability issues -Transportation to doctor's office -Applying for or appealing denial of Social Security disability benefits -Estates and trusts -Becoming the guardian of a person with a disability -Out-of-state issues -Workers' compensation -Medical malpractice or personal injury -Consumer or bankruptcy issues -Anything that is not the wish of the person with the disability 900 SW 5th Avenue Suite 1800 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Individuals with disabilities who have legal issues related to their disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-11am and 1pm-4pm Email (503) 243-2081 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING BURNS INFORMATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides technical assistance, training modules, and dissemination of disability-related information for individuals, groups, and Centers for Independent Living that focus on the advancement of the global Independent Living Movement. 51 W Washington Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 370-2810 401 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING LA GRANDE Movement. 1021 SW 5th Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 370-2810 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR individuals, groups, and Centers for Independent Living that focus on the advancement of the global Independent Living Movement. 322 SW 3rd Avenue Pendleton, OR 978012174 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 370-2810 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING THE DALLES INFORMATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides technical assistance, training modules, and dissemination of disability-related information for individuals, groups, and Centers for Independent Living that focus on the advancement of the global Independent Living Movement. 400 E Scenic Drive The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 370-2810 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL REHABILITATION INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a library and database with books, research, journals, and videos about disabilities, education, rehabilitation, employment, independent living, assistive technology, and related laws and policies. Staff can help with reference questions, search for specific disability-related topics, provide access to bibliographic databases, and supply photocopies of materials that may be hard to find elsewhere. The website also includes searchable databases with disability and rehabilitation resources. 8400 Corporate Drive Suite 500 Landover, MD 20785 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 5:30am-2:30pm, closed federal holidays Email (800) 346-2742 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAAST NETWORK SAMMY'S PLACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral about support networks, programs, and community agencies relating to the topic of intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Website Nehalem, OR 97131 Eligibility: Individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities and their friends and family members Hours: Not applicable Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COUNCIL ON DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on disability-related resources and groups. Resources include opportunities for trainings, consultations, and advocacy/self-advocacy, financial support for disability-related trainings and conferences, and advocacy for public policy affecting people with disabilities. Programs are for families and individuals affected by disability, direct care providers, case workers, and others. 2475 SE Ladd Avenue Suite 231 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (971) 204-8824 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK MID-OREGON REGION OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 4263 Commercial Street SE Suite 300 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK NORTH OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 7110 SW Fir Loop Suite 160 Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK SOUTH OREGON REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 72A Centennial Loop Suite 150 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON SCREENING PROJECT DEVELOPMENTAL SCREENING QUESTIONNAIRES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Confidential website where parents and caregivers can check a child’s development by completing an online questionnaire. No personal identifying information is required. Results letters, completed questionnaires, learning activities, follow-up resources, parenting materials, and community referrals are available by email or download. The site also provides contact information for Oregon Early Intervention providers and a Developmental Screening Toolkit for professionals who support families. A project coordinator monitors the site daily and is available to parents, caregivers, and providers by phone or email. Website Eligibility: Parents and primary caregivers of children ages 1 month-6 years Hours: Not applicable Email (541) 346-2580 Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES SENIORS AND PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES INTELLECTUAL AND DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administrative offices for Oregon’s statewide system of county ODHS offices serving adults and children with intellectual or developmental disabilities. Provides information about available services and directs callers to their local office. County Community Developmental Disability Program (CDDP) offices determine eligibility. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Children and adults with intellectual/developmental disabilities, and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 945-5811 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE ARC JACKSON COUNTY DIRECT SUPPORT PROFESSIONAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance, information, and resources on community social services online or over the phone. 311 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 283-7529 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UCP OREGON ADULT COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, information and referral, residential and vocational supports, brokerage support, and supported employment. The Supported Living Program provides daily living assistance, including live-in care. Support can include medication administration, financial paperwork and budgeting assistance, assistance with chores and cooking, and social supports. 96000 NE Cascades Parkway Suite 400 Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who are experiencing cerebral palsy or other intellectual and developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4:30pm Email (503) 777-4166 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE AMERICANS WITH DISABILITIES ACT INFORMATION LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an information line for general and technical questions concerning ADA issues (Americans with Disabilities Act) such as public accommodations, design standards, and enforcement. Also provides topic specific information on the website including information related to service animals. 950 Pennsylvania Avenue NW Washington, DC 20530 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 6:30am-9am and noon-2:30pm, Thursday 11:30am-2:30pm Email (800) 514-0301 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ACCESS OUTREACH TO SENIORS AND PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES ARC PORTLAND METRO CLUB IMPACT ARC UMATILLA COUNTY ARC UMATILLA COUNTY CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER CITY OF PORTLAND 311 CITY AND COUNTY INFORMATION AND REFERRAL CLEARVIEW DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER DIRECTION SERVICE INFORMATION AND REFERRAL DISABILITY RIGHTS OREGON DISABILITY RIGHTS OREGON EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES OREGON COAAST NETWORK SAMMY'S PLACE OREGON COUNCIL ON DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES INTELLECTUAL AND DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES THE ARC JACKSON COUNTY DIRECT SUPPORT PROFESSIONAL PROGRAM UCP OREGON ADULT COMMUNITY SERVICES
